title: ceratolacis erythrolichen
intro: Ceratolacis erythrolichen, also known as Red Coral Bush, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Ceratolacis erythrolichen are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
